item.title: Google told staff it is ‘proud’ of Pentagon AI contract after internal backlash
item.description: The tech giant signed an artificial intelligence deal with the defence department on Monday

item.title: Meta shares drop as it boosts AI spending to $145bn this year
item.description: Google, Microsoft and Amazon report strong cloud computing growth

item.title: UK ticket tout ban set to be delayed for a year
item.description: Only draft legislation is likely to be included in next month’s King’s Speech

item.title: Musk says he was ‘a fool’ to fund the launch of OpenAI
item.description: Billionaire says Sam Altman wanted ‘halo effect’ of a non-profit while enriching himself in second day of his testimony

item.title: Trump’s Iran war has cost $25bn, Pentagon official says
item.description: Use of costly missiles and air defence interceptors raises alarm about US readiness for other conflicts

item.title: Build data centres in Scotland not London, says Britain’s energy grid boss
item.description: Comments by Fintan Slye come as government seeks to increase capacity to power AI boom

item.title: Franco-German tank maker launches probe into bribery allegations ahead of €20bn listing
item.description: KNDS is gearing up for dual listing in Frankfurt and Paris as early as this summer

item.title: The twilight of Opec
item.description: UAE’s departure leaves Saudi Arabia trying to hold together a fractious cartel

item.title: GSK’s game of catch-up might just work
item.description: Drugmaker is looking to see which of its pipeline of drugs can be pushed forward while also trying to buy time

item.title: Wild Scottish salmon catch drops by 40% in one year
item.description: New data highlights ‘extremely concerning’ decline in fish population

item.title: Uber adds hotel booking with Expedia in ‘super app’ push
item.description: CEO Dara Khosrowshahi says deal will help the ride-hailing group sell paid memberships

item.title: EU warns Hungary and Slovakia on fuel price discrimination
item.description: Differential pricing to protect local motorists at expense of foreigners infringes EU law, says Brussels

item.title: UBS’s bumper profit both helps and hinders its case on capital rules
item.description: Long fight over just how well fortified its balance sheet needs to be is inching in Swiss bank’s direction

item.title: The problem with falling house prices
item.description: Homes are too expensive in the UK, but a large correction would be bad for everyone — including first-time buyers

item.title: France leads way in plans to shift away from fossil fuels at summit
item.description: Macron government sets out so-called roadmap ‘with a clear deadline’

item.title: AstraZeneca revives Cambridge investment after UK agrees to pay more for drugs
item.description: Resumption of £300mn in UK projects is a result of trade deal with US that aims to increase medicines spending

item.title: Australia’s drive to get more blood flowing
item.description: The country has more recreational beekeepers than regular donors and is forced to rely on imports

item.title: German and Spanish inflation rises less than expected in April
item.description: Softer price pressures in large Eurozone economies strengthen case for ECB to hold interest rates

item.title: UAE’s Opec exit will drive down oil prices, warns Moscow
item.description: Russia and Kazakhstan confirm their intention to remain within Opec+

item.title: Chinese wind turbine maker accuses UK of ‘politicisation’ over product ban
item.description: Ming Yang warns setback will not stop company’s expansion plans in Europe

item.title: Mercedes challenges FCA motor finance compensation scheme
item.description: Finance arm of carmaker among three lenders to appeal regulator’s £9.1bn redress programme

item.title: Kazakhstan’s ex-deputy PM describes UK company as ‘criminal enterprise’ at tribunal
item.description: Yerbol Orynbayev makes allegation against financial services group Jusan on second day of hearing in London

item.title: China’s CIC considers selling Heathrow stake over third runway cost worries
item.description: Beijing-backed wealth fund puts 10% stake in London airport on ‘active watch’

item.title: Artificial Intelligence in the Real Economy: A Visual Guide
item.description: A new series of visual explainers looking at how AI is transforming different industries.

item.title: Lululemon founder casts doubt on new chief as proxy fight escalates
item.description: Chip Wilson argues appointing Heidi O’Neill to top job could lead CEO search to be ‘examined with a refreshed board’

item.title: Why electricity markets are stuck on the gas price rollercoaster
item.description: Governments have no easy options to remove the link between gas and power prices

item.title: The Proxies 2025: Where are they now?
item.description: Perk to the future

item.title: DCC considers takeover bid from KKR and Energy Capital
item.description: Shares in FTSE 100 company surge more than 14%

item.title: BoE cracks down on insurers’ offshore pension transfers
item.description: Regulator to change capital rules on ‘funded reinsurance’ over concerns sector is exploiting arbitrage

item.title: How OpenAI’s $500bn data centre venture Stargate has shifted shape
item.description: Chief executive Sam Altman’s flexible approach to infrastructure projects is unsettling partners but boosting computing lead
